title: Cliff wampa

The '''cliff wampa''' was a [[Color/Legends|brown]]-[[Hair/Legends|furred]], rock-climbing [[Species/Legends|subspecies]] [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineered]] from the [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] [[Water/Legends|ice]] creature native to the frozen [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. After learning of the ice wampa's powerful ferocity during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] captured several of the creatures, and Imperial [[Biologist/Legends|biologists]] artificially adapted them to various environments. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], the Empire had placed the resulting cliff wampa on the rocky Outer Rim [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Gall/Legends|Gall]] to guard local [[Cadavine Sector Fleet Imperial Enclave|Imperial interests]].

==Biology and appearance==
A product of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineering]], the cliff wampa was a manufactured [[Species/Legends|subspecies]] of the [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] [[Water/Legends|ice]] creature indigenous to the ice [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Whereas the ice wampa was covered with a coat of [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] for camouflage on Hoth's frozen plains, the cliff wampa was gray or light brown in color to blend in with the rocky habitat of the Imperial-controlled [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Gall/Legends|Gall]]. The cliff wampa boasted sharp, [[Iron/Legends|iron]]-strong claws capable of climbing or digging through solid rock, much like the Hoth wampa would dig through ice.<ref name="NEGAS" />

==Behavior and intelligence==
Like its Hoth cousin, the cliff wampa was [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] in nature.<ref name="NEGAS" /> Cliff wampas were temperamental and hungry, and would maul anything or anyone they encountered.<ref name="Insider 33">{{InsiderCite|33|Wampa Attack!}}</ref>

==History==
The origin of the cliff wampa began during the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] tenure at the [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] facility on the<ref name="NEGAS" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> ice planet Hoth. The Rebels of Echo Base endured repeated assaults on their encampment from Hoth's native wampas.<ref name="NEGAS" /> During one such attack, the Rebels neutralized and incarcerated several of the creatures, holding them in secluded pens within the base.<ref name="GG3">[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]]</ref>

Later, during the Imperial assault on Echo Base in the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], several of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] elite [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtroopers]] were slaughtered by the Rebels' caged wampas after charging blindly into one of the creatures' holding pens inside the base.<ref name="One Step Ahead">{{WEGCite|story=One Step Ahead|book=Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)}}</ref> Imperial [[Biologist/Legends|biologists]] were so impressed with the powerful wampa<ref name="SOTE Limited Collector's Edition">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Limited Collector's Edition|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Limited Collector's Edition]]</ref> after receiving reports of how the creatures terrorized Echo Base<ref name="NEGAS" /> that the Empire managed to capture some of the beasts and, through bioengineering, adapted them to survive in various environments as guard animals. The cliff wampa was a result of the Empire's efforts.<ref name="Insider 33" />

==Cliff wampas in the galaxy==
By [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], just [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Battle of Hoth,<ref name="Date2">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]]. This [[Time/Legends|time]] period covers the cliff [[Wampa/Legends|wampa's]] presence on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Gall/Legends|Gall]], as presented in the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' video game]].</ref> the Empire installed the cliff wampa to guard<ref name="NEGAS" /> the [[Cadavine Sector Fleet Imperial Enclave]] on the rocky Outer Rim moon of Gall.<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook">''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> The [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]], who had previously come face-to-face with the Rebels' captured ice wampas within the bowels of Echo Base during the Battle of Hoth, encountered a single cliff wampa while attempting to [[Battle of Gall|infiltrate]] the [[Imperial Enclave]] through the high cliffs of [[Smuggler's Canyon]] in a failed effort to rescue the captive [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] from the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].<ref name="SOTE">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' video game]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|We also thought that it would be fun to sneak a wampa into another level of the game where he's hiding in a rock cliff on the moon Gall. We colored him tan to camouflage him and hid him inside a cave […] The cliff wampa is actually the result of Imperial bioengineering. The Empire was so impressed by this powerful creature, who you'll remember did maul several of their vaunted stormtroopers on Hoth, that they managed to capture some and adapt them to various environments to be used almost like a vicious guard dog.|Jon Knoles, ''Shadows of the Empire'' video game creator|<ref name="Insider 33" />}}
The cliff wampa was created for the [[1996]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'']].<ref name="SOTE" /> According to ''Shadows of the Empire'' game creator [[Jon Knoles]], as recounted in the [[Star Wars Insider 33|''Star Wars Insider'' 33]] article [[Wampa Attack!|''LucasArts takes on the Wampa'']], the [[LucasArts]] game developers inserted a rock-camouflaged wampa into the "[[Gall City|Gall Spaceport]]" level as a way to have fun with the game<ref name="Insider 33" />—developer [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]] is jokingly listed in the game credits as "Cliff Wampa Zoologist."<ref name="SOTE" /> The article likens the cliff wampa's appearance in ''Shadows of the Empire'' to any number of hidden tricks, or "[[Easter egg (virtual)|Easter eggs]]," that game designers have always created to "stretch the fun factor" but noted that LucasArts developers tried to be conservative with what secrets they included in their games, knowing that anything they created could later appear in other ''[[Star Wars]]'' works and thus become official [[canon]] when it was never meant to be.<ref name="Insider 33" />

The 1996 [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Limited Collector's Edition|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Limited Collector's Edition]] strategy guide for the ''Shadows of the Empire'' video game, compiled by [[Crispin Boyer]], was the first source to identify the "Gall [[Spaceport/Legends|Spaceport]]" creature as a "cliff wampa."<ref name="SOTE Limited Collector's Edition" /> Jon Knoles described LucasArts' original backstory for its "fabled" creature as the result of Imperial bioengineering,<ref name="Insider 33" /> information later fully canonized in the [[2006]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''.<ref name="NEGAS" /> In both [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Game Secrets|''Shadows of the Empire: Game Secrets'']] and [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: The Official Strategy Guide|''Shadows of the Empire: The Official Strategy Guide'']], published by [[Prima Games]], writer [[Bart Farkas]] colloquially refers to the cliff wampa as a "'''mountain wampa'''."<ref name="SOTE Game Secrets">''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Game Secrets]]''</ref><ref name="SOTE Official Strategy Guide">''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>

==Appearances==
